exampleSentencesTranslated: I always keep hope that things will get better.
She feels hope when she hears the new information about her job.
He carries a hope for a bright future in his heart.

example.en: I always long for my homeland.
example.vi: Tôi luôn vọng về quê hương.

definitions.en: to long for; to hope for something that may not happen
definitions.es: anhelar; esperar algo que puede no suceder
definitions.vi: mong mỏi; hy vọng điều gì đó có thể không xảy ra

exampleSentences: Em luôn giữ vọng rằng mọi chuyện sẽ tốt đẹp hơn.
Chị cảm thấy có vọng khi nghe tin mới về công việc.
Anh mang trong lòng một vọng về tương lai tươi sáng.
notes:
Vọng means hope or aspiration, but by itself it sounds formal or poetic. In real life, you’ll usually see it inside compound words.

It’s an abstract noun, so you don’t count it directly. Common combinations: hy vọng (hope), kỳ vọng (expectation), tuyệt vọng (despair), vô vọng (hopeless), thất vọng (disappointed — not the same as losing hope completely).

Think of vọng as the “hope” core inside bigger words.  
Strong memory link: hy vọng (positive hope) ↔ tuyệt vọng (no hope at all).

translation.en: I heard an echo from afar.

content.sentence: Tôi nghe thấy tiếng ___ từ xa.
Tôi nghe thấy tiếng ___ từ xa.

translation.en: I heard an echo from afar.

notes_md:
**Vọng** means *hope* or *aspiration*, but by itself it sounds formal or poetic. In real life, you’ll usually see it inside compound words.

It’s an abstract noun, so you don’t count it directly. Common combinations: **hy vọng** (hope), **kỳ vọng** (expectation), **tuyệt vọng** (despair), **vô vọng** (hopeless), **thất vọng** (disappointed — not the same as losing hope completely).

Think of **vọng** as the “hope” core inside bigger words.  
Strong memory link: **hy vọng** (positive hope) ↔ **tuyệt vọng** (no hope at all).
